reasoning: The baseline 30-day average over the prior year is just over 74 protests per month. The threshold needed to resolve YES is more than 10x the baseline, making it roughly 752 protests recorded by ACLED in a single 30-day window. This would demand an astounding average of ~25 reported protests per day across the nation, an unprecedented level of civil unrest. While large-scale movements do elevate protest numbers, a 1000% jump over the standard baseline is extremely rare for a peaceful and established democracy like Australia. The upcoming 2025 Federal Election on May 3 could loosely elevate political mobilization during the April 29 window, but fundamentally falls short of causing the magnitude required. I've scaled the tail-end probabilities up slightly to account for the uncertainty inherent in longer forecast horizons, per instructions, but the likelihood remains exceedingly low.
